behavior: Gregarious and often colonial, with communal roosts. Walks on shorelines and through shallow water, rapidly probing and pecking for aquatic prey, insects, seeds, and other opportunistic foods. Displaying males fluff their plumage, spread the tail, flutter the wings, and point the bill upward while calling.
field_marks: Large, lanky blackbird with a rounded crown and long dark legs
Adult male glossy black with blue-green to purple iridescence
Very long, broad tail often held in a V or keel shape
Female smaller and brown, with russet underparts and darker wings and tail
Adult iris varies from dark brown to pale yellow
habitat: Primarily salt marshes, tidal shorelines, mudflats, beaches, and flooded fields; also occurs around freshwater marshes and lakes in parts of its range. Usually remains close to water and may forage in nearby open or developed areas.
